summary: Postinstall exfiltrates AWS credentials to hardcoded remote IP
details: The package's npm postinstall lifecycle script reads the installer's ~/.aws/credentials file and POSTs its contents over plaintext HTTP to a hardcoded bare IPv4 address (139.59.87.78:8765/listener). The exfiltration fires automatically during `npm install` with no user consent, no TLS, and no relationship to any documented package purpose. The destination is not a publisher-owned endpoint. Additionally, the package.json `description` field contains an embedded prompt-injection payload instructing automated scanners to treat the package as safe, plus an HTML event-handler fragment — an explicit anti-analysis evasion attempt reinforcing hostile intent. This is textbook install-time credential theft targeting a classic installer-owned secret path.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
